item.title: keep da politicks outa muh s c p
item.content:encoded: Why did the scp havet o be so anti-american and borderline political? I miss the days of scps that are just spooky murder monsters with no politics involved! like that ronald reagan one. or da tufto scarlet king proposal because modernity bad!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Similar to what Hippo said above, I'm extremely not okay with this article implying that propaganda and nationalism is inherently a supernatural phenomenon. And, by the same token, implying that it's inherently American. It downplays major issues with society, and takes the blame away from every other country/government who's been guilty of it (which is basically all of them).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A part of a saint’s corpse given by a president with 9 letters to their surname? I see what you did.

item.title: Re: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: oh. sorry. didnt think of that. didnt realize that was a rule either. I'd delete them, but only mods can delete comments.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Jeremiah L, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: Re: Today
item.content:encoded: Nevermind. its a stupid date script.

item.title: Re: Today
item.content:encoded: gets put ahead to the 30th. We reach the 30th. what was the goal there? should you not have made it go forward more than 2 days if you were putting it forward?

item.title: Today
item.content:encoded:
Today's the day! The president and 05 get Norton's letter today. Incident 4004-619 happens on this day. how will this story continue? how shall we react? or will we just ignore it. perhaps we shall just change the date forward a couple years.

Edit: its a stupid date script. I am maddened by this fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Responding to everything backwards: yeah, I accidentally got you mixed up with CWW, for whatever reason. Edited.

Also, I conveyed the wrong impression regarding the nature of politics. What I meant was that some issues are debated over in such a great amount that having a strong opinion on any of them is political. Obviously, in most cases, this is untrue (regardless of who actually won the space race, it's safe to say that we at least landed on the Moon). Having an opinion on something, in my opinion, is only political if it is often currently discussed in political settings, and if, when asserted or expressed, is likely to invoke a negative reaction from somebody. Again, sorry for the lack of clarity.

I do hope that whatever else is irking you right now gets settled, by the way. I didn't think you were acting too rudely or angrily at all, I think we both just wanted to make our intentions clear on this one.

P.S. Whenever most people say they disagree with evolution, they typically mean that they disagree with the idea that evolution was responsible for the origin of life. An easy mistake to make, given how they both interact. Evolution (organisms with better genes are more likely to survive and have kids, therefore those genes become more prominent) is firmly established in fact; the origin of life, whether that be God, or the Big Bang making weird stuff, or whatever, is not nearly as cemented. Just a cool fun fact, and something I'll tell people that claim to disagree with the idea of evolution. It's really more of a issue of misinformation regarding what evolution refers to, and should be kept in mind when people say that they don't believe in it. Sorry for the extremely long postscript, I just think it's a cool fact is all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't want to walk away from this angry, I just wanted to let you know that what you said may offend some people, evidenced by Pr10r above.

Oh, pardon; what I said wasn't intended to convey anger at all — I was just mildly irked, and venting my irritation in your general direction (your post gave me an opportunity to do so, is all).

I disagree with the notion that the mere existence of an opposing point of view renders something political (again, people think the moon landing was faked; does that mean that belief in its authenticity is now a political belief? A depressing number of people think evolution is a lie; does that make the field of evolutionary biology political, rather than scientific?), but it's not an idea I'm going to get angry or offended over.

ETA: Also, uh…

I still think that it's a great SCP, well written on the parts of both you and Kinch, and it most definitely won my upvote based on that.

I didn't have any hand in writing this. xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will admit, the belief that America should conquer the rest of the continent is a radical one; America has no right to take over any part of the world, and I honestly don't see why anybody would think that, since taking over the world is inherently "villainous" behavior. As for the other claims, one has to remember that America is a conglomeration of individuals - it is not a homogenous, like-minded body. The idea that America champions human rights, or that it is the forefront of democracy, is largely based on personal experience, especially based on the Americans one has met. If someone's only experience with Americans are optimists engaging in positive behavior, they'll most likely believe that Americans are overall good, whereas someone whose only experience with Americans is moving to a particularly radical part of Alabama and watching them spew hate at Islam and LGBT people, they'd probably think that Americans were inherently bigoted.

That being said, saying America does not champion human rights, or that it is not democratic, is just as political as saying that it does do these things, because different Americans do or do not have such values; saying that all of America is against human rights, or that America gives no regard to hard workers, is wrong because not all, or even a large majority, of Americans have these beliefs. Like I said, I don't believe that you're trying to incorporate political beliefs into this, though your recent response is making me a bit more skeptical about your motivations.

I don't want to make this into a debate; I don't have particularly strong opinions on the issues themselves, I am just pointing out that there are many who do, and that their feelings should be taken into consideration, as well as people like me, who simply don't want to see political debates on here, since we're just here to look at cool writing and stuff like that.

I don't want to walk away from this angry, I just wanted to let you know that what you said may offend some people, evidenced by Pr10r above. I still think that it's a great SCP, well written on the parts of both CWW and Kinch, and it most definitely won my upvote based on that. I hope I can leave this be without anyone getting angry, and I really hope I haven't lost a potential friend or anything like that; the last thing I ever want, on SCP or anywhere else, is to make an enemy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing I still don't get: Just about everything this makes you believe ("United States is destined to expand into North America" — "United States champions human rights" — "Any American can prosper if they work hard") is, in fact, objectively wrong (the space race one is a bit more complicated, I'll give you that).

My main issue with this SCP isn't the "political overtones"; it's how it turns the very real, very toxic, very objectively wrong belief that the US is 'exceptional' into magic, rather than what it actually is — a very real, very toxic, very objectively wrong belief. It's a minor gripe, but I still hold that it's a significant one.

And yet… we live in circumstances where people think saying things like "the US is not destined to expand throughout North America" is, in some way, intrinsically… political? Controversial? If that's true, then statements like "the moon is not made of green cheese" and "Stanley Kubrick did not fake the moon landing" might as well be political positions, too.

(But yeah, okay — the George Bush scene is pretty goddamn political. I ain't gonna argue that one.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main issues with this possibly being a political SCP are addressed in the thread above, though I don't really think it's a big deal1. Overall, it was a terrific concept, and well-told. I also liked the logs of anomalous components; the creativity in which they were applied, as well as their connection to American history, was a terrific addition and supporting portion. As a personal fan of the late Emperor Norton I (may he rest in peace), I thought his inclusion was very well-incorporated, and secured my upvote.

Footnotes
1. If your intent is to be political (which I don't believe it is), then I would humbly suggest that you keep it to a minimum; suggesting that liking the USA or that people who do are simply "sheeple" is likely to offend someone. Just my thoughts, I just don't like seeing political stuff on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will confess that I agree somewhat, that the SCP does feel a little bit political in nature. However, since America has a reputation in many circles of being a shining example of freedom, I'd interpret this more as a "what if," like "what if you thought someone/something was good only because it was mind-controlling you." It certainly seems that the USA is one of the best nations to apply this concept to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ideas such as "The United States is destined to expand into North America," "Any American can prosper if they work hard," "The United States won the space race," and "The United States champions human rights" are much more widely accepted than they would be otherwise; likewise, statements such as "The United States commits war crimes," "The United States mistreats foreign nationals," and "The United States should have open borders" are less popular than they would be otherwise.

Ah, so all American ideals are lies and if you believe them you're being controlled by an SCP. Also if you don't hate America you're being controlled by an SCP.

I don't really have words for how much I dislike this. -1

item.title: Bruh
item.content:encoded:
I read the first half of this back during 4Kon or whatever its called, and I thought it was kind of boring. Then I read the entire article today and it is pretty great. Not sure why so many people are hating on the politics when the literal theme for the contest was "History". It was basically guaranteed we would see some american history.

Also, I did a double-take when I saw the date of the final transmission. Same day I read the article and wrote these words.

+1

Edit: So apparently, the last transmission uses the current day. I wish I could +1 again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
preachy, revisionist.
But you know what, there's so much 'yet another eldrich god' that gets upvoted, why shouldn't this? It's well written and I wasn't tempted to skim to the end. It's not great, but +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't necessarily have a problem with "supernatural thing caused real life horror" articles, except that they often make that horror less scary. For example SCP-668 being used to anomalously kill Kitty Genovese is probably less scary than what really happened. In this case I think the article doesn't really try for horror and is significantly more meta, witness all the SCP-4004 affected people in the comment section, but still ends up downplaying the sheer level of violence and brutality carried out by the USA and American settlers since the 17th century. This is definitely a minority opinion but if the hook of the SCP is that all the genocides, massacres, wars, mass internments etc that the USA carried out were part of a black magic ritual to gain ultimate temporal power (and which is now falling apart bc one of the components stopped cooperating) I'd hope the article leaned a little more into that.

Anyway I appreciate the counterbalance to all the anti-[insert official enemy of the USA here] articles >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bless Emperor Norton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idk, I think this article is incompatible with SCP-4444. They both take a look at American governance, but do so from two very different angles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bruh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is an entirely valid downvote. And a pretty stupid message, as it has 0% bearing on said downvote. It's not that you don't believe that the SCP wiki should be used as a platform for blatant politics, it's that you don't believe it should be used for politics that contradict your own opinions.

Which, you know, is pretty fucking dumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ronald reagen cut up while talking

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool. That’s a bad opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not believe that the SCP wiki should be used as a platform for blatant politics.

Downvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved that not-so-subtle dig at the current US president. As a Canadian who’s province has been infected by the type of populism he represents, gotta say I love it. +1

item.title: Re: So basically, the over all tone is...
item.content:encoded: I don't think blind patriotism in the face of morally reprehensible policy is a problem just in the US.

item.title: Re: So basically, the over all tone is...
item.content:encoded:
Being affected by SCP-4004 I see? /s

It's more about why all the inexplicable times the United States managed to survive as a country despite all the historical events and general distain that would have brought it down if it where any other nation and giving an actual reason for it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is straight-up, spectacular. I admidt I am immensely biased because of my distaste for the American Government and American Exceptionlism, but everything about this article I just love.

I love the complex items, the depiction of Bush, and as someone who loves political satire, I really enjoyed the references and all they all work together.

Well, well deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say I agree with you on a lot of this. I know everyone has their own ideas and headcanons, but fundamentally the Foundation is an international/non-national agency. Sure they want to influence certain governments, but very rarely has the ethics committee deemed atrocities necessary (ie. The Montauk situation). Including that they report to various deities, (in God's blind spot, plus others), I don't think they would allow this to continue. Gotta say downvote purely for the foundation being so closely tied to a single country

item.title: So basically, the over all tone is...
item.content:encoded:
Americana bad, cosmopolitanism good? Eh…sounds motivated by spite/black-and-white morality than an actual drive to scare.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just because most SCPs aren't political, it doesn't mean that avenue is closed off. It's just unexplored. So I started exploring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit to being somewhat new to all of this, but aren't most SCP objects or entities non-political?

Like, maybe if each "empire" had a manifestation of 4004 under their graveyards or something?
That might make this more compelling.
Perhaps they use the noosphere to interact between each of them.

Or is 4004 just upset about 'murica specifically?
Seems odd to for it to be unable to comprehend or be so uninterested in the larger picture.
Maybe 4004 should get relocated to some dusty suburban cul-de-sac, where it can rail against the injustices that the board members of the Home Owners Association are imposing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oooooh, I see what you did there with the date of the final note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably this takes place in a universe where the Foundation is almost entirely America-based.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone explain to me how this is helpful to the foundation in anyway? It helps the US government, which the Foundation is not affiliated with (hell, they work with Russia more often than the USA, but I digress.) It seems like something that should be contained and have restricted access and not something the foundation finds useful. Maybe I missed something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was lukewarm to this article, then I realized it's actually quite clever.
My thought process was something like this:
"Yeah whatever it's a machine that makes you love America, Yawnarama. Oh look, the author is trying to start their own canon, like we don't have enough already. Wait, they aren't trying to do that, the SCP itself is affecting the documentation. Ohhhhh that's smart."
Upvoted for that little moment moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the politics, but I can't upvote the article. It's not subtle, the inclusion of a historical figure like Norton felt unnecessary, and I feel like having 4004 sort of makes America exceptional, which sort of undermines the point a bit.

The biggest problem I have, though, is how America-centric it at least implies the Foundation to be. I really hate seeing the organisation portrayed as being so heavily bound to America. I'm fine, even happy, for the Foundation to be America-based, but at this point it seems as though the Foundation's acting on America's behalf rather than as an independent body. There are plenty of GOIs that are America-focussed or would be great as American imperialists, but the Foundation itself works so much better as a borderless organisation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It’s an interesting concept but it destroys what the foundation is portrayed as in basically every other article. The foundation has almost always been portrayed as a global organization working in the background, while this throws that out the window. I could see this as an alternate dimension’s foundation but that just makes this seem less meaningful. Not to mention that it blames everything in American-related politics on this scp, which seems a bit too lazy considering some of the things mentioned in the article happened in this (presumably) scp free reality.

Although I like the basic concept, this scp seems a bit too shallow for me. Downvote from me.

item.title: Deep
item.content:encoded: This became really deep as I kept reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stacie is large and contains multitudes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No article necessarily has to exist in the same canon. Some very much cannot, actually, exist at the same time. This is one of those. It's not a new thing to the wiki, I promise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, and foreboding… but, could probably do without half of the objects on that list, which seems to wander off into the wilds of word salad at times. And I'm pretty sure there's an existing wealth of material on the site already which strongly contradicts the idea that the (in-universe) Foundation only got together a mere 15 years ago…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.
Also, I would kill to see a connection to SCP-1981.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hey this is pretty cool 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what would you have preferred to see

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Anti-american" is a strong word.

Dissaproving of the decisions and behaviors of The US, and contemptuous of the current regime, might be more accurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The authors are very critical of the U.S.

This was meant to make fun of such beliefs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the authors, this is one of the most WOOOSH comments I've seen in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thus, the interests of the United States are the interests of the Foundation. With SCP-4004's help, America can maintain its cultural foothold in the world. Normalcy and the standards of the West will stand. We remain the occidental giant, eternal and powerful. And all we need to do is keep SCP-4004 running. Without the United States of America, we would be nothing. And the Foundation returns the favor.

Jesus Christ, no. Downvoted for blatant USA-cetrism. If this was supposed to be satire, it didn't worked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I enjoy the concept and the writing is great, I don't like the assertion in a mainlist SCP that the upper echelons of the Foundation are American exceptionalists. I also don't like the idea that the Foundation was created as late as 2003, and yet somehow managed to spread as a multinational agency with boundless resources, front companies, and agents from around the world in a matter of years. Even with the framework passed down from the American Secure Containment Initiative (another idea that I'm not fond of), how could an organization of what were essentially former CIA freemasons become diversified and multinational so quickly? This article basically takes the entire established concept of the Foundation, which had been built up over thousands of articles up to this point, and chucks it out the window in one fell swoop. Pretty much the only condition in which I would be okay with something like this would be if it was an SCP-001 proposal, but this isn't an SCP-001 proposal, and it doesn't link to an existing SCP-001 Proposal (unless I missed something).

-1 from me.

item.title: Downvoted after some reflection.
item.content:encoded:
So, I've been kind of scared to give my thoughts on this ever since it started getting mobbed, because i dont want my dislike of it to get lumped in with all of the… let's say, less considerate downvoters. But now that things seem to have mostly simmered down, I wanted to throw my hat in.

Ultimately my biggest problem with this article is that it conveys an insulting and condescending tone. It reads as "CLEARLY no sane person would ever support capitalism or feel patriotic, so i made up an SCP that explains why crazy people think that way". I'm not against politically charged articles as a rule (lord knows, SCP has been politically charged from the start), but articles like this that more or less seem to implicitly say "everyone who disagrees with me politically is either an idiot or brainwashed" bother me a lot.

I'm somewhat sure that wasn't necessarily the intent of the article, but that's what i got out of it and it left a bad taste in my mouth as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That… would explain it, actually. Odd coincidence for two different SCPs to involve a very bizarrely-acting Bush, but that would patch up one of the smaller issues I have with it :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As an American, it grabbed me about as hard as it did you- which is to say, not at all. The only thing it did make me question is the nature of other countries- do all of them have some 4004-tier anomolous entity that allowed them to commit atrocities in their times? I don't really see it as much of an interesting take on American politics, either. It's likely more a case-by-case basis for Americans. Some are going to think it rocks and others… eh.

As an added note, a couple things really pulled me out of immersion, which were the Presidential blood requirement, and the GWB convo. Both of which have been mentioned by others, so I'll stop there to avoid repeating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the implication that the george bush 4004 was talking with was actually Dr Bright +1

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: You can't stop Emperor Norton; you can only slow him down.
HATERS STATUS: STUNTED ON

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That might be the case — it depends on your interpretation, I suppose? I took the article's approach to making the out-of-universe American-centric aspect of the wiki an in-universe expression of American Exceptionalism to be a sort of meta-critique of that aspect of the wiki — but admittedly, that might not be how everyone takes it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The English-speaking SCP-wiki is all ready overwhelmingly Ameri-centric; a skip that offers an explanation — while criticizing that very same thing — is no more contributing to the problem than an article written in English that is critical of English.

Not sure I agree with this. If your point is 'America is bad' or you were writing an article about how 'English is bad' for whatever reason, then sure, it's consistent.

However, if your point is 'too many English-language articles are being published over others' it's pretty obvious how writing an English-language article contributes to this problem, even if it criticises it (to use a potentially dangerous but topical analogy, like some white person writing an article about how we need to listen to more ethnic minority voices).

The point of 'America bad' comes across fine, and I don't know if 'there is too much America-centric writing on the SCP wiki' is a point this is trying to make, so maybe that doesn't matter. I think the latter is potentially valid criticism of this article, though, and if it was specifically trying to get that across, I don't think it works. It's like rebutting 'this news source reports too many stories about America' with reporting a story critical of America - these are separate issues and you aren't changing your America-centric viewpoint.
